title: salix mongolica var. suchowensis
intro: Salix mongolica var. suchowensis, also known as Mongolian willow or Suchow Mongolian willow, is a plant species belonging to the genus Salix, commonly found in northeastern China and Korea. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful for treating various health conditions. The plant's health benefits include reducing fever, relieving pain, and soothing respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. Salix mongolica var. suchowensis is often prepared as a tea or used in herbal infusions, and its dried bark and leaves are also used in traditional Chinese medicine.
